name: sim_phase_damping_fixed_point_geometry
classification_note: Canonical bounded coupling row for the phase-damping fixed-point manifold: it ties a dissipative channel family to a geometric fixed set and isolates its Hopf-compatible pure-state intersection without broadening into general carrier or channel taxonomy claims.

pytorch.reason: load_bearing: sweep p in [0,1], apply phase_damping to diag(p,1-p), measure fixed-point distance; confirm off-diagonal state is NOT fixed

pyg.reason: not attempted; not applicable to this sim family

z3.reason: load_bearing: UNSAT proofs (N1: no non-diagonal fixed point of phase damping; N2: amplitude damping has no 1-d fixed-point manifold); SAT proof (P6: poles |0⟩ and |1⟩ are simultaneously Hopf-stable and PD-fixed)

cvc5.reason: not attempted; z3 is sufficient for the required SAT/UNSAT checks

sympy.reason: load_bearing: analytic proof that K0 diag(p,1-p) K0† + K1 diag(p,1-p) K1† = diag(p,1-p) for arbitrary p and gamma; verifies entire diagonal is fixed symbolically

clifford.reason: load_bearing: model the fixed-point manifold as the e3 direction in Cl(3); verify e3 generates the Z-axis (grade-1 vector); Bloch sphere embedded in even subalgebra; fixed-point line = e3 axis

geomstats.reason: load_bearing: SPD geodesic from I/2 to diag(p,1-p) for p in [0,1]; compute geodesic distances and sectional curvature probe along the fixed-point manifold

e3nn.reason: not attempted; no equivariant network layer is part of this fixed-point geometry row

rustworkx.reason: not attempted; no graph-routing or DAG structure is needed here

xgi.reason: load_bearing: simplicial complex of fixed-point structure across channel families; 0-simplices = individual fixed points; 1-simplex = phase-damping Z-axis line; compute Betti numbers beta0, beta1

toponetx.reason: not attempted; no cell-complex object is needed for this row

gudhi.reason: not attempted; no persistent-homology computation is part of this row

P1_pytorch_diagonal_sweep.conclusion: ALL diagonal states diag(p,1-p) are exact fixed points of phase damping for all p in [0,1] and all gamma in (0,1). The fixed-point set is a 1-parameter family (line segment) in state space.

P2_sympy_analytic_proof.conclusion: ANALYTICALLY PROVEN: K0 diag(p,1-p) K0† + K1 diag(p,1-p) K1† = diag(p,1-p) for arbitrary symbolic p and gamma. The entire diagonal manifold is fixed.

P3_clifford_e3_axis.fixed_point_manifold_cl3: span{e3} = the e3 axis (grade-1 subspace of e3)
P3_clifford_e3_axis.phase_damping_kills: e1 and e2 components (off-diagonal = x,y Bloch)
P3_clifford_e3_axis.conclusion: The phase-damping fixed-point manifold = span{e3} in Cl(3). Phase damping is a projection onto the e3 axis. The fixed-point manifold is a 1-dimensional subspace of the grade-1 subspace of Cl(3), corresponding to the Z-axis of the Bloch sphere.

interpretation.beta0=1: All fixed points are in one connected component (|0⟩ is the hub)
interpretation.beta1=0: No topological holes — the fixed-point graph is a tree (path), no loop
interpretation.no_2simplex: |1⟩ is NOT a fixed point of amplitude damping or depolarizing; therefore {|0⟩, |1⟩, I/2} do not form a 2-simplex. Phase damping is topologically distinct: it has an edge {0,1} that no other channel possesses.

P4_xgi_simplicial_complex.conclusion: The fixed-point simplicial complex has beta0=1, beta1=0, beta2=0. The phase-damping 1-simplex {|0⟩,|1⟩} is the unique edge not shared by other channel families. Topologically, the complex is contractible (homotopy equivalent to a point), with no holes or voids.

P5_geomstats_curvature.conclusion: The SPD metric along the diagonal fixed-point manifold has VARYING ratio (non-trivial curvature along the Z-axis in SPD metric). The affine-invariant SPD metric is NOT flat on the full manifold (SPD(2) has negative curvature), but the diagonal submanifold may have non-trivial induced curvature.

constraints.hopf_stable: Tr(rho²)=1 (pure state, lies on S² = Hopf base)

P6_z3_sat_poles_hopf_and_pd.conclusion: SAT CONFIRMED: States satisfying BOTH phase-damping fixed-point (b=c=0) AND Hopf-stability (pure state, Tr(rho²)=1) are EXACTLY a=0 (|1⟩) and a=1 (|0⟩). The intersection of the phase-damping fixed manifold and the Hopf pure-state constraint isolates exactly the two poles of the Bloch sphere.

N1_unsat_no_nondiagonal_fixed_point.sat_claim: There exists a non-diagonal state (b≠0) that is a fixed point of phase damping for gamma in (0,1)

encoding.contradiction: b≠0 and b*s=b implies s=1, contradicts s<1

N1_unsat_no_nondiagonal_fixed_point.conclusion: UNSAT CONFIRMED: No non-diagonal 2×2 state can be a fixed point of phase damping for any gamma in (0,1). Off-diagonal elements must be zero. The fixed-point manifold is EXACTLY the set of diagonal states.

N2_unsat_amplitude_damping_no_manifold.sat_claim: Amplitude damping has a fixed diagonal state diag(a,1-a) for a in (0,1) (interior of the Z-axis), i.e., a 1-d fixed-point manifold

encoding.fp_condition: a + g*(1-a) = a (fixed-point for AD on diagonal)
encoding.simplifies_to: g*(1-a) = 0; with a<1 and g>0: no solution

N2_unsat_amplitude_damping_no_manifold.conclusion: UNSAT CONFIRMED: Amplitude damping has NO interior fixed diagonal states. Its only fixed point is |0⟩ (a=1), a unique fixed point, NOT a manifold. This formally distinguishes amplitude damping from phase damping.

B1_boundary_poles_fixed.conclusion: |0⟩ and |1⟩ are both exact fixed points of phase damping (trivially, as they are diagonal with no off-diagonal component to decay). They are the endpoints of the 1-simplex fixed-point manifold.

B2_off_diagonal_not_fixed.conclusion: |+⟩⟨+| is NOT a fixed point of phase damping: off-diagonal elements decay from 0.5 to 0.3536 (factor sqrt(1-gamma) = 0.7071). Only DIAGONAL states are fixed. |+⟩ is NOT in the fixed-point manifold.

B3_interior_manifold_verification.conclusion: ALL interior diagonal states (p in (0,1)) are fixed under phase damping for all tested gamma values. The fixed-point set is genuinely a 1-d manifold, not just isolated points.

fixed_point_manifold.description: All diagonal states diag(p,1-p), p in [0,1]
fixed_point_manifold.cl3_object: e3 axis (grade-1 e3 subspace of Cl(3))
fixed_point_manifold.geomstats: 1-d submanifold of SPD(2) with affine-invariant metric

hopf_intersection.claim: PD fixed-point manifold ∩ Hopf pure-state constraint = {|0⟩, |1⟩}
